Category: Finance & Professional Services: Banking, Insurance, Accounting, Consulting, Legal, Real Estate
Who is this for: Financial analysts, budget managers, accounting professionals, bank managers, insurance underwriters, finance directors, business analysts, financial planners
Team or Dept this is for: Finance, Accounting, Risk Management, Operations, Banking, Insurance
Description: Overconfidence in predictions and model limitations.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with financial concepts such as budgeting or forecasting, along with a general curiosity about how artificial intelligence is used in business, is all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key limitations of AI-driven financial forecasting tools and explain how overconfidence in model predictions can lead to costly errors in banking, insurance, and accounting contexts. You will also apply practical strategies to critically evaluate AI-generated forecasts, recognise the warning signs of model bias, and communicate uncertainty to colleagues and clients with greater confidence.
